chapter_title.ar: باب بَدْءِ الْأَذَانِ وَ الْإِقَامَةِ وَ فَضْلِهِمَا وَ ثَوَابِهِمَا
chapter_title.en: Chapter 18 – The Azān (Call to the Salāt), and the Iqamah (Call to the establishment of the Salāt), and their merits and their Rewards

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (5/90)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (5/90)
text: قَالَ أَبُو عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) يَا أَبَا هَارُونَ الْإِقَامَةُ مِنَ الصَّلَاةِ فَإِذَا أَقَمْتَهُ فَلَا تَتَكَلَّمْ وَ لَا تُومِ بِيَدِكَ .

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Muhammad Bin Al Husayn, from Muhammad Bin Ismail, from Salih Bin Uqba, from Abu Haroun Al Makfouf who said,
‘Abu Abdullahasws said: ‘O Abu Haroun! The Iqamah is from the Salāt. So when you establish it, so neither speak nor gesture with your hands’.
en.sarwar: 20. Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated from Muhammad ibn al-Husayn from Muhammad ibn ’Isma‘il from Salih ibn ‘Uqbah from abu Harun al-Makfuf who has said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘O abu Harun, ‘Iqamah is part of Salah (prayer). When you say it do not speak or point with your hands.’”
